AddBucketItemRequest.description:
Body for Adding a Bucket item under one item type.

>>> AddBucketItemRequest(
...     item_type="movie", data={"title": "Dune"}, intent_context="recommended"
... ).item_type
'movie'
AddBucketItemRequest.title: AddBucketItemRequest

AddBucketItemResponse.description: The Add result: the new item plus its dedup advisory.
AddBucketItemResponse.title: AddBucketItemResponse

AgentModelRead.description: HTTP representation of one enabled model choice.
AgentModelRead.title: AgentModelRead

AnswerOutcomeRead.description: The result of answering: the grading, the rescheduled prompt, completion.
AnswerOutcomeRead.title: AnswerOutcomeRead

AnswerPromptRequest.description:
Body for answering a recall prompt, shaped by the prompt's kind.

Multiple choice sends `selected_index`; short answer sends `answer_text`;
essay sends `answer_text` plus the human-confirmed `confirmed_correct`
(ADR 0004 — the model only ever proposes an essay grade). `response_ms`
always rides along to refine the SM-2 quality.

>>> AnswerPromptRequest(selected_index=0, response_ms=1200).selected_index
0
AnswerPromptRequest.title: AnswerPromptRequest

ArtifactEventRead.description: HTTP representation of one artifact event.
ArtifactEventRead.title: ArtifactEventRead

ArtifactRead.description:
Full HTTP representation of one artifact version, `html` included.

Served by the REST reads (latest / by version) the browser fetches to
mount an artifact's sandboxed iframe.
ArtifactRead.title: ArtifactRead

ArtifactSummaryRead.description: Overview HTTP representation of an artifact's latest version, no `html`.
ArtifactSummaryRead.title: ArtifactSummaryRead

BucketItemRead.description:
HTTP representation of a Bucket item, exposing its derived `state`.

>>> read = BucketItemRead(
...     id="018f0000-0000-7000-8000-000000000000",
...     item_type="movie",
...     state="active",
...     title="Dune",
...     data={"title": "Dune"},
...     intent_context="recommended",
...     version=1,
...     created_at=datetime(2026, 1, 1),
...     updated_at=datetime(2026, 1, 1),
...     completed_at=None,
...     deleted_at=None,
... )
>>> read.state
'active'
BucketItemRead.title: BucketItemRead

CacheMeta.description:
Whether a result was served from the local cache or fetched live.

>>> CacheMeta(hit=False, source="live").source
'live'
CacheMeta.title: CacheMeta

CaptureRequest.description:
Body for capturing a loose Memory.

>>> CaptureRequest(content="I prefer aisle seats").content
'I prefer aisle seats'
CaptureRequest.title: CaptureRequest

CompleteRequest.description:
Body for completing a Bucket item at an observed `version`.

>>> CompleteRequest(version=1).version
1
CompleteRequest.title: CompleteRequest

ConversationRead.description:
HTTP representation of a host-owned conversation.

`session_gap_seconds` and `latest_activity` let the frontend compute
whether the *next* message will land on a fresh pi session (see
`ConversationService.resolve_session`) without hardcoding the gap.
ConversationRead.title: ConversationRead

CreatePanelRequest.description: Body for creating a Synthetic panel.
CreatePanelRequest.title: CreatePanelRequest

CreateTriggerRequest.description: Body for creating a Scheduled trigger.
CreateTriggerRequest.title: CreateTriggerRequest

DecayedIntentContext.description: A stale item's immutable *why*, paired with how far it has eroded.
DecayedIntentContext.title: DecayedIntentContext

DedupAdvisoryRead.description:
The dedup advisory returned alongside a freshly Added item.

`severity` is `warn` when an active duplicate already exists, `inform` when
the only duplicates are terminal (completed/deleted), `none` otherwise.
Dedup never blocks, so the item is Added regardless.
DedupAdvisoryRead.title: DedupAdvisoryRead

DuePromptRead.description: An outstanding prompt plus the study item it belongs to.
DuePromptRead.title: DuePromptRead

DuplicateCluster.description: Two or more active items that share one identity (item type + dedup key).
DuplicateCluster.title: DuplicateCluster

EditRequest.description:
Body for editing a Memory's content at an observed `version`.

>>> EditRequest(content="I prefer window seats", version=1).version
1
EditRequest.title: EditRequest

EssayGradeProposalRead.description:
The model's proposed essay grade, for the human to confirm or override.

`proposed_correct` is `null` when the model was unavailable — the rubric is
still revealed so the human can grade unaided. Nothing is scheduled by a
proposal; only the confirmed answer reaches SM-2 (ADR 0004).
EssayGradeProposalRead.title: EssayGradeProposalRead

FusedSearchResultRead.description:
One fused, source-tagged Search result.

Exactly one of `memory` / `bucket_item` is populated, matching `source` —
a discriminated shape so a heterogeneous result list is self-describing
without a second round trip.

>>> read = FusedSearchResultRead.from_hit(hit)
>>> read.source
'bucket_item'
FusedSearchResultRead.title: FusedSearchResultRead

LoginRequest.description: Body for app login with the shared password.
LoginRequest.title: LoginRequest

MemoryRead.description:
HTTP representation of a Memory, exposing its derived trust `state`.

>>> read = MemoryRead(
...     content="I prefer aisle seats",
...     created_at=datetime(2026, 1, 1),
...     facets={},
...     id="018f0000-0000-7000-8000-000000000000",
...     state="loose",
...     tethered_at=None,
...     updated_at=datetime(2026, 1, 1),
...     version=1,
... )
>>> read.state
'loose'
MemoryRead.title: MemoryRead

MessageRead.description: HTTP representation of a settled transcript row.
MessageRead.title: MessageRead

ModelListRead.description: HTTP response containing the curated allowlist and global default.
ModelListRead.title: ModelListRead

NotificationRead.description: HTTP representation of a persisted notification.
NotificationRead.title: NotificationRead

PanelRead.description: HTTP representation of a Synthetic panel.
PanelRead.title: PanelRead

PanelResultsRead.description: One panel execution: the capped rows plus the uncapped match count.
PanelResultsRead.title: PanelResultsRead

PostArtifactEventRequest.description:
Body for relaying one artifact event.

`payload` is opaque, free-form JSON — the `postMessage` payload a
sandboxed artifact posted to its parent, relayed verbatim under the
browser's own session; no schema is enforced beyond "a JSON object".

>>> PostArtifactEventRequest(payload={"type": "answer", "value": 3}).payload
{'type': 'answer', 'value': 3}
PostArtifactEventRequest.title: PostArtifactEventRequest

ProposeEssayGradeRequest.description:
Body for requesting a model-proposed essay grade to confirm.

>>> ProposeEssayGradeRequest(answer_text="An essay.").answer_text
'An essay.'
ProposeEssayGradeRequest.title: ProposeEssayGradeRequest

PushStatusRead.description: HTTP representation of the browser's push-subscription status.
PushStatusRead.title: PushStatusRead

PushSubscriptionRead.description: HTTP representation of a stored push subscription.
PushSubscriptionRead.title: PushSubscriptionRead

QuotaMeta.description:
The day's quota budget snapshot a guarded call reports.

>>> QuotaMeta(limit=100, used=3, remaining=97).remaining
97
QuotaMeta.title: QuotaMeta

RecallPromptRead.description:
HTTP representation of a recall prompt, with the grading payload withheld.

`correct_index`, `reference_answer`, and `rubric` are deliberately absent:
the client renders and answers the prompt without being able to read the
answer key off the wire (the rubric surfaces only in the essay
grade-proposal step, after the essay is written).
RecallPromptRead.title: RecallPromptRead

SessionResponse.description: Whether the request carries a currently valid app session.
SessionResponse.title: SessionResponse

SetConversationModelRequest.description: Body for selecting a conversation's model.
SetConversationModelRequest.title: SetConversationModelRequest

SourceUsageRead.description:
HTTP representation of one transcript source's own metered-use budget.

Distinct from `quota` (the YouTube Data API's per-day budget, shared by
every source that calls it): a source with its own cap (e.g. Supadata's
monthly budget) reports one of these, keyed by its source name on
`YouTubeSyncStatusRead.usage`. `period` is the UTC calendar month
(`YYYY-MM`) Supadata's `used`/`remaining` apply to; a source with no
natural period concept leaves it empty.
SourceUsageRead.title: SourceUsageRead

StaleItem.description: An active item old enough to reconsider, with its decayed intent context.
StaleItem.title: StaleItem

StartRecallRequest.description:
Body for promoting an ingested educational video into a study item.

>>> StartRecallRequest(video_id="v1").video_id
'v1'
StartRecallRequest.title: StartRecallRequest

StudyItemRead.description: HTTP representation of a study item.
StudyItemRead.title: StudyItemRead

SubscribeRequest.description:
Body for registering a browser push subscription.

>>> SubscribeRequest(endpoint="https://push/abc", p256dh="k", auth="a").endpoint
'https://push/abc'
SubscribeRequest.title: SubscribeRequest

TetherRequest.description:
Body for tethering a Memory at an observed `version`.

>>> TetherRequest(version=1).version
1
TetherRequest.title: TetherRequest

TranscriptProviderPauseRead.description: HTTP representation of a transcript source paused by an IP block.
TranscriptProviderPauseRead.title: TranscriptProviderPauseRead

TriageReport.description: The full Triage view of the active Bucket list at one point in time.
TriageReport.title: TriageReport

TriggerRead.description: HTTP representation of a Scheduled trigger.
TriggerRead.title: TriggerRead

UnderSpecifiedItem.description: An active item whose payload lacks the detail to act on it later.
UnderSpecifiedItem.title: UnderSpecifiedItem

UnsubscribeRequest.description: Body for removing a browser push subscription.
UnsubscribeRequest.title: UnsubscribeRequest

UpdatePanelRequest.description: Body for replacing a panel's definition at an observed version.
UpdatePanelRequest.title: UpdatePanelRequest

UpdateTriggerRequest.description: Body for replacing a trigger's definition at an observed version.
UpdateTriggerRequest.title: UpdateTriggerRequest

VoiceCaptureResponse.description:
A transcribed voice note and the loose Memory it captured.

>>> VoiceCaptureResponse(
...     memory=MemoryRead(
...         content="buy oat milk",
...         created_at=datetime(2026, 1, 1),
...         facets={"source": "voice"},
...         id="018f0000-0000-7000-8000-000000000000",
...         state="loose",
...         tethered_at=None,
...         updated_at=datetime(2026, 1, 1),
...         version=1,
...     ),
...     transcript="buy oat milk",
... ).transcript
'buy oat milk'
VoiceCaptureResponse.title: VoiceCaptureResponse

YouTubeSyncStatusRead.description:
HTTP snapshot of the background ingestion's progress and health.

>>> read = YouTubeSyncStatusRead(
...     videos_total=3,
...     transcripts_done=1,
...     transcripts_pending=1,
...     transcripts_unavailable=1,
...     last_synced_at=None,
...     quota=QuotaMeta(limit=10, used=0, remaining=10),
...     api_paused_until=None,
...     transcript_providers_paused=[],
...     usage={},
... )
>>> read.videos_total
3
YouTubeSyncStatusRead.title: YouTubeSyncStatusRead

YouTubeTranscriptResponse.description: A fetched transcript: the updated video, its text, and quota + cache.
YouTubeTranscriptResponse.title: YouTubeTranscriptResponse

YouTubeVideoListResponse.description: A browse/search result: the videos plus the call's quota + cache.
YouTubeVideoListResponse.title: YouTubeVideoListResponse

YouTubeVideoRead.description:
HTTP representation of an ingested video, exposing its derived state.

>>> read = YouTubeVideoRead(
...     id="018f0000-0000-7000-8000-000000000000",
...     video_id="v1",
...     source="liked",
...     state="active",
...     title="Talk",
...     channel="PyConf",
...     topic="python",
...     description="",
...     transcript=None,
...     created_at=datetime(2026, 1, 1),
...     updated_at=datetime(2026, 1, 1),
...     ignored_at=None,
... )
>>> read.state
'active'
YouTubeVideoRead.title: YouTubeVideoRead

get.summary: List every artifact's latest version as lightweight summaries.
Fetch an artifact's newest version, `html` included.
List an artifact's events, oldest first.

post.summary: Append one free-form event to an artifact's log.

get.summary: Fetch one specific past version of an artifact, `html` included.

post.summary: Authenticate with the app password and set a session cookie.

get.summary: Report whether the request carries a valid app session.
List Bucket items in a lifecycle state (active list / retained history).

post.summary: Add a Bucket item; the response carries its dedup advisory.

get.summary: Keyword Search over active Bucket items.
Compute the read-only Triage report over the live active Bucket list.

delete.summary: Delete a Bucket item, moving it to terminal history.

post.summary: Complete a Bucket item, moving it to terminal history.
Transcribe an uploaded audio note and capture it as a loose Memory.

get.summary: List host-owned conversations.

delete.summary: Clear one conversation's transcript and rotate its pi session.

get.summary: List settled transcript rows for one conversation.

post.summary: Select the model used for subsequent turns in one conversation.

get.summary: Filter the review queue (`loose`) or browse the corpus (`tethered`).
Keyword Search over tethered Memories.

patch.summary: Edit a Memory's `content`; a human edit keeps trust.

post.summary: Promote a loose Memory to tethered.

get.summary: List host-enabled agent model choices.
List undismissed notifications, newest first.
List live Synthetic panels in position order.

put.summary: Replace a panel's definition at an observed version.

get.summary: Run a panel's saved query, recomputed against the corpus right now.
Report whether this browser (or any browser) is subscribed.

post.summary: Register (or refresh) this browser's push subscription.

get.summary: List the recall prompts currently owed a review (the outstanding surface).

post.summary: Answer a recall prompt, grading and rescheduling it (tethering on completion).
Propose a model grade for an essay answer, for the human to confirm.

get.summary: List every study item, newest-first.

post.summary: Promote an ingested educational video into a study item under Recall.

get.summary: Cross-source Search: RRF-fused Memory + Bucket-item arms, source-tagged.
List live Scheduled triggers, soonest next fire first.

put.summary: Replace a trigger's definition, re-arming it from its next occurrence.

get.summary: List active ingested videos, optionally filtered by topic and source.
Keyword Search across saved content and transcript text.
Report the background ingestion's progress and health (local read only).

post.summary: Fetch and persist a transcript for an ingested video.
